front: Who is Greta M. Ljung and what is her primary contribution to time series analysis?
back: Greta M. Ljung is a Swedish statistician and professor. She is best known for co-developing the Ljung-Box test (with George E.P. Box), a statistical test used to check for autocorrelation in a time series.

front: What is the null hypothesis (H₀) of the Ljung-Box test?
back: The null hypothesis of the Ljung-Box test is that the data are independently distributed, meaning there is no significant autocorrelation in the series up to the specified number of lags.

front: What is the formula for the Ljung-Box Q-statistic?
back: Q = n(n+2)∑(ρₖ²/(n-k)) from k=1 to h, where n is the sample size, ρₖ is the sample autocorrelation at lag k, and h is the number of lags being tested.

front: When is the Ljung-Box test most commonly applied in time series modeling?
back: It is primarily used for model diagnostics, specifically to test whether the residuals from a fitted ARIMA (or similar) model are white noise (i.e., uncorrelated). A non-significant p-value suggests the model has adequately captured the structure in the data.

front: How does the Ljung-Box test improve upon the earlier Box-Pierce test?
back: The Ljung-Box statistic has better small-sample properties. It is a modified version that is more accurate and powerful for smaller sample sizes by using a weighted sum of squared autocorrelations.
